page: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote storage attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32s_v7 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests and PassMark CPU scores. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks are highly efficient, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reaching top-tier status in the top 10%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU physics and encryption tests also register strong results in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and overall memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks remain in the average tier.

The server exhibits distinct performance tradeoffs, particularly in large-scale workloads. While prompt processing for small and medium language models is strong, text generation for large 70B models falls into the weak category, reflecting the lack of hardware accelerators. Qualitative cost efficiency is balanced, offering a standard resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU suitable for general-purpose workloads. This configuration is appropriate for static web serving, Redis database operations, and moderate-scale data processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It provides 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 128.0 GB of system memory, with no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, alongside top-tier single-threaded gzip and bzip3 processing. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. While prompt processing for small and medium language models is strong, text generation for large 70B models is weak due to the absence of hardware acceleration. This server is suitable for static web serving, Redis operations, and general-purpose enterprise workloads utilizing remote Premium Storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for remote Premium Storage.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, strong single-core performance, and support for remote premium storage.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier single-threaded compression
No local storage, relying on remote Premium Storage capabilities
Suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and general-purpose workloads
